mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden jump in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals acute labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when the unemployment rate jumps above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a regression coefficient on the interaction term statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and rising input costs, compressing profit margins and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the predictive slope flips positive.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3% signals deflationary pressure that forces central banks to cut rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL drops by more than 0.3%, or the signal loses predictive power after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 60-day SPX returns remains below 0.01, or the correlation turns negative during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and accelerating retail sales indicates unsustainable consumption debt, which triggers a mean-reversion sell-off and negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable housing starts during a post-recession recovery phase signals robust capacity utilization and inventory restocking, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns, with a hit rate below 45% in walk-forward testing.
